title: Senior Associate - Strategy&
description:
Strategy& – Senior Associate – Strategy Consulting

Sectors – Consumer || FMCG || Industrial || FMEG || E-Commerce || Retail

Location – Mumbai || Gurgaon




Senior Associates at Strategy&, part of the PwC network, typically bring 3–5 years of experience and have demonstrated strong consulting or corporate strategy exposure across sectors and problem statements. They play a critical role in solving strategic challenges, working shoulder-to-shoulder with clients to address their most pressing issues — from growth and market entry to digital transformation and business model innovation.

Senior Associates are expected to contribute to the development of corporate and business unit strategies, evaluate market opportunities, structure problems, conduct analyses, and support end-to-end project delivery. Common areas of focus include Corporate Strategy, Growth Strategy, Go-to-Market Design, Market Entry, Digital Strategy and Transformation Projects.

Key Attributes and Abilities We Seek

As a Senior Associate, you will be expected to demonstrate analytical thinking, initiative, collaboration, and high personal drive. PwC Professional skills and responsibilities at this level include, but are not limited to:

Strong problem structuring and solving capabilities (quantitative and qualitative), and the ability to apply structured thinking across different industries and contexts.
Demonstrated ability to translate complex, ambiguous business issues into clear problem statements with actionable insights.
Conducting and synthesizing market research, primary interviews, competitive benchmarking, and data analysis to derive impactful recommendations.
Collaborating effectively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment, providing and seeking regular feedback and clarification.
Supporting engagement leadership in project planning, workstream management, and client communication.
Drafting high-quality deliverables and client-ready presentations in a clear, structured, and insightful manner.
Engaging with client teams to understand business context, validate findings, and co-create solutions.
Driving internal initiatives and contributing to knowledge sharing and practice development.

What We Look For

We look for exceptional individuals who combine strong consulting fundamentals with intellectual curiosity, business acumen, and a collaborative mindset. Our ideal candidates demonstrate:

Strong academic and professional achievement; an MBA from a top-tier institution.
Experience in strategy, business planning, transformation, or business development roles in top-tier consulting firms or corporate strategy teams.
Strong analytical, communication, and storytelling skills, with the ability to distill insights from data and clearly articulate strategic narratives.
A reputation for problem-solving, creative thinking, and ability to connect the dots across business functions.
The ability to work with multiple stakeholders, handle ambiguity, and adapt in dynamic client situations.
A natural inclination toward collaboration and mentorship, with emerging leadership qualities.
High levels of ownership, self-motivation, and attention to detail.
Passion for a consulting career — being on the front line of solving real-world business problems.

What You’ll Bring (Experience & Qualifications)

3–5 years of experience in strategy consulting or relevant corporate roles.
1-3 years of experience in consumer, retail or industrials sector.
An MBA from a top tier institution.
Practical exposure in one or more of the following areas:
Corporate and Business Unit Strategy
Growth Strategy, Market Entry, Go-to-Market Design
Organizational or Digital Transformation
Business development, marketing, or sales strategy
Strong command of Excel, PowerPoint, and other analytical tools to structure and communicate insights.
Ability to adapt across industries and business issues, showing a solid understanding of market dynamics and enterprise levers.
Demonstrated experience in leading or co-leading workstreams and contributing to impactful client outcomes.




Sectors - Consumer || FMCG || Industrial || FMEG || E Commerce - Location - Mumbai || Gurgaon

title: Customer Service Associate
description:
Company Overview

Reliance Retail is India's leading and fastest-growing retailer, renowned for its omni-channel presence, superior product quality, and exceptional customer experiences. With a vast network across 7,000+ cities and innovative digital platforms, Reliance Retail is committed to delivering outstanding value and service to millions of customers nationwide.

Job Overview

The Customer Service Specialist role at Reliance Retail is a full-time, entry-level (Fresher) opportunity based in Jaipur. The selected candidate will be responsible for delivering exceptional customer support, efficiently addressing queries, and helping maintain our reputation for service excellence. Applicants with 0 to 1 year of work experience are welcome to apply.

Qualifications And Skills


Customer Support (Mandatory skill): Ability to handle questions, complaints, and requests professionally and with consistent courtesy, ensuring continuous customer satisfaction and loyalty.
Communication Skills (Mandatory skill): Exceptional verbal and written communication skills to articulate solutions clearly and build rapport with diverse customers in real time.
Active Listening (Mandatory skill): Capable of listening attentively to customer concerns, accurately understanding needs, and responding with pertinent solutions to fulfill expectations.
Time Management: Demonstrated skill in prioritizing tasks, managing multiple customer requests, and meeting deadlines within a fast-paced retail environment.
Product Knowledge: Willingness to continually enhance knowledge of Reliance Retail's offerings to provide accurate guidance and detailed product information to customers.
Microsoft Office: Familiarity with using basic Microsoft Office tools such as Word, Excel, and Outlook to document interactions and facilitate internal communication efficiently.
Problem Solving: Ability to identify core issues, think critically, and deliver prompt, practical solutions that resolve concerns while adhering to company policies.
Adaptability: Capable of adjusting to changes in processes, technology, and customer expectations, supporting the dynamic nature of the retail sector.


Roles And Responsibilities


Respond promptly to customer inquiries through various channels including phone, email, and in-store interactions, ensuring a positive customer experience at all times.
Resolve product or service complaints by clarifying customer concerns, determining the cause, selecting and explaining the best solution, and following up to ensure resolution.
Maintain up-to-date knowledge of products, promotions, store policies, and procedures to accurately assist customers and address their needs efficiently.
Document customer interactions, transactions, comments, and complaints accurately in Microsoft Office systems for internal records and analysis.
Collaborate closely with fellow team members and other departments to escalate issues and achieve optimal resolutions in a timely manner.
Provide feedback on recurring customer issues and suggest potential improvements to enhance service quality and operational processes.
Support the store team in achieving overall service standards, compliance, and customer-centric goals as laid out by Reliance Retails philosophy.
Continuously work to build product knowledge and stay aware of latest trends, services, and updates within the retail sector.

title: Intern - Marketing, Brands and Communications
description:
Enabling Areas – Marketing, Brand, and Communications

Marketing, Brand, and Communications – Intern, Mumbai (3-6 months)

What impact will you make?

Every day, your work will make an impact that matters, while you thrive in a dynamic culture of inclusion, collaboration and high performance. As the undisputed leader in professional services, Deloitte is where you’ll find unrivalled opportunities to succeed and realise your full potential

Work you’ll do:

Within the Marketing, Brand, and Communications team, your work will be aligned with marketing and engagement initiatives. This entails ideation and execution of marketing campaigns for change and business initiatives across various communication channels.




Essentials:

Support digital marketing and campaign planning initiatives while contributing to storytelling and marketing communication efforts
Being proactive with fresh ideas and taking ownership to execute campaigns and marketing strategy
Staying updated with the latest innovations and trends in marketing and social media communications
Comfort with producing short format format-friendly and engaging content from end-to-end
Strong language skills, both verbal and written
Strong skills in PowerPoint, Canva and any other creative tools to be able to support on visualising and creating periodic progress reports for the team




Qualifications:

A graduate degree in marketing, mass media, or similar fields is an advantage, as are additional certifications in digital marketing-related programs. However, we are open to anyone with a creative bent of mind.
Should have completed bachelors at least.
Currently in Mumbai and will to travel to Thane




Experience:

Prior experience in digital marketing, branding, social media marketing or similar is mandatory.




Your role as a leader

At Deloitte India, we believe in the importance of leadership at all levels. We expect our people to embrace and live our purpose by challenging themselves to identify issues that are most important for our clients, our people, and for society and make an impact that matters.




In addition to living our purpose, Executive across our organization:

Builds own understanding of our purpose and values; explores opportunities for impact
Demonstrates strong commitment to personal learning and development; acts as a brand
ambassador to help attract top talent
Understands expectations and demonstrates personal accountability for keeping performance on track
Actively focuses on developing effective communication and relationship-building skills
Understands how their daily work contributes to the priorities of the team and the business




How you’ll grow

At Deloitte, our professional development plan focuses on helping people at every level of their career to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day. From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe there’s always room to learn. We offer opportunities to help build world-class skills in addition to hands-on experience in the global, fast-changing business world. From on-the-job learning experiences to formal development programs at Deloitte University, our professionals have a variety of opportunities to continue to grow throughout their career.

title: Scrum Master I
description:
Exhibits the fundamental qualities of a servant leader and demonstrates the fundamental principles and benefits of the Agile framework in an effort to improve team performance in the areas of quality, predictability, flow and velocity. Assists teams in meeting their delivery goals through enforcing team working agreements; communication techniques, coordination and cooperation; identifying and eliminating impediments; and ongoing coaching and development.

Acts as a servant leader for a single team and stakeholders. Aids the team and Product Owner in efforts to manage and sequence the backlog. Leads less complex teams with work contained with limited external team impact or dependencies. Works generally with internal systems involving few system integration points requiring occasional support from senior level Scrum Master. Eliminates impediments and resolves issues with frequent support from Release Train Engineer or other resources. Resolves and manages dependencies with frequent guidance from Release Train Engineer or other resources to support team improvement and promotes high performance. Manages team performance in the areas of quality, predictability, flow and velocity and identifies SAFe anti-patterns and resolve with frequent support. Utilizes beginner coaching capabilities to influence the team and help coach on practices and procedures. With frequent support from RTE guides the team through the decision making process and teach techniques on how to problem solve, improve communication, self-manage and self-organize. With frequent support from RTE Scrum Master, builds a trusting and safe environment where problems can be raised. Help manages interpersonal conflicts, challenges and opportunities for growth within the team. Participates, facilitates and manages the outputs of all agile ceremonies including daily Stand Up meetings, Sprint review, sprint r etrospective, sprint planning, backlog refinement sessions, prepare for Program Increment Planning Sessions. Participates in the Scrum Mastery Sync Sessions and Agile Release Train Sync Sessions. Demonstrates ongoing desire for knowledge and continuous improvement by pursuing continuous education with regards to everything Agile (e.g. visit user groups, attend conferences, read books, write blogs, etc.). Continuously works with the Scrum Master Community of Practice to ensure standard procedures are in place and ultimately improve the organizations' product delivery capability. Seeks advice and works closely with other Scrum Masters and Release Train Engineers to establish best practices and resolve challenging issues, risks, conflicts or impediments. Performs other duties as assigned.

Requirements

Communication skills - Intermediate

Team Facilitation skills - Intermediate

Software / Tool Skills

Microsoft Tools - Entry Level (1-3 Years)

Rally / Jira Experience - Entry Level (1-3 Years)

Usage of AI tools - Entry Level (1-3 Years)

Benefits

Competitive compensation and benefits package:


Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Comprehensive benefits package
Career development and training opportunities
Flexible work arrangements (remote and/or office-based)
Dynamic and inclusive work culture within a globally renowned group
Private Health Insurance
Pension Plan
Paid Time Off
Training & Development


Note: Benefits differ based on employee level.

About Capgemini

Capgemini is a global leader in partnering with companies to transform and manage their business by harnessing the power of technology. The Group is guided everyday by its purpose of unleashing human energy through technology for an inclusive and sustainable future. It is a responsible and diverse organization of over 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong 55-year heritage and deep industry expertise, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to address the entire breadth of their business needs, from strategy and design to operations, fueled by the fast evolving and innovative world of cloud, data, AI, connectivity, software, digital engineering and platforms. The Group €22.5 billion in revenues in 2023.

https://www.capgemini.com/us-en/about-us/who-we-are/

title: Supervisor/Executive F&A
description:
Responsible for managing key financial operations including revenue recognition, project accounting, working capital monitoring, statutory compliances, audit support, MIS reporting, and financial controls. The role ensures accuracy, transparency, and timeliness of financial data essential for management decision?making and statutory obligations.

Key Responsibilities1. Revenue & Cost Accounting


Preparation and review of RSV (Revenue) statements, RSV entry reversals, and RSV interest workings.
Monitoring of Project Valuation Reports, performance variance, and completed project cost provisions.
Analysis of OI/OB sales, PLR (Price Level Reserve) provision, and DLP (Defect Liability Period) provision.
Ensuring accurate project cost capitalization including AUC details and CWIP statement.


2. Working Capital & Operational Finance


Preparation of Working Capital reports and monitoring of receivables/payables.
Tracking project-wise unadjusted advances and reconciliation with the project team.
Verification of Cash Flow workings and supporting treasury functions.
Compilation of Yard Details and inventory?related financial impact.


3. MIS, Reporting & Management Presentations


Development of Labour Dashboard, Fixed Cost Report, Capacity Utilization details, and other MIS reports for management review.
Preparing Audit Committee data, management decks, and Deloitte PPT support.
Analysis of Forex Exposure and reporting to management.


4. Compliance, Audit & Controls


Supporting statutory, internal, and external audits including ICFR documentation, RPT (Related Party Transaction) reporting, and balance confirmations.
Ensuring Compliance of Wages through ECS and other labour/finance?related statutory requirements.
Preparation of ECL (Expected Credit Loss) entries as per financial standards.
Monitoring Asset Disposal accounting and documentation.


5. Special Projects & Cross?Functional Coordination


Maintenance and review of Master Project financial data.
Tracking Orders Lost & Orders Deferred and coordinating with sales/commercial teams.
Contributing to CLCS reporting, PAT built?up, and other strategic financial analyses.
Coordination with departments for validating data for financial controls and reporting.


Core Competencies


Strong financial analysis and accounting knowledge
Proficiency in project accounting
Excellent MIS preparation & presentation skills
Understanding of audit & compliance frameworks
Ability to work cross?functionally with project, operations, and commercial teams
Attention to detail and strong reconciliation skills

title: Data Quality Analyst
description:
We invite you to come grow with us.

Inizio Ignite is the global advisory partner for health and life sciences, encompassing connected expertise across Research Partnership, Putnam, Vynamic, and STEM. Guided by purpose, passion, and precision, we collaborate with clients to ignite impact, accelerate performance, and deliver lasting change for patients. By uniting strategy, insights, and innovation with a unique depth and breadth of expertise, we drive transformation and shape the future of health.

Our Research Partnership healthcare market research team provides research and insights to inform and power healthcare decisions. Serving a variety of clients across the lifecycle, our expertise includes data, research & insights (proprietary syndicated and custom research) and brand tracking (ATU). Proudly part of Inizio – the market-leading commercialization partner for health and life sciences. From early-stage development to loss of exclusivity, we simplify complexity, unlock value, and make collaboration seamless at every pivotal moment – supporting confident, insight-driven decisions that move innovation forward.

About the role:

The Data Quality Analyst role will work alongside colleagues in Europe, India and Singapore on major Brand Equity tracking projects for a global client covering multiple Therapy Areas across a number of international markets. The position will report into the Program Data Manager, based in the UK, with direct colleagues in analytics based in India and the UK. This is a high profile client and project for our business, providing a platform for the project team to have a significant impact on the client’s brand strategies.

The role will involve working with our Research project teams, the Data Management & Dashboards team and with analytics experts to investigate and check data across multiple workstreams for our major tracking programs, primarily using Python.

About the team:

The Gurugram hub office is part of our global team, partnering with colleagues across different functional areas and specialisms. This is an exciting period of growth for Inizio Ignite | Research Partnership, as we expand into the fast-growing Indian market.

Your primary role:

As Data Quality Analyst, you will support the Program Data Manager to ensure data quality and consistency throughout the reporting cycle on our Major Tracking programs. You will also support with advising the Research project teams and Data Management & Dashboard teams based in Europe, India and Singapore.

Technical Expertise


Python (3 to 6 years) experience for data processing, automation, and pipeline support
Experience managing end-to-end data pipelines (ingestion, transformation, storage, outputs)


Database Expertise


Strong SQL skills using PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MS SQL
Experience with data modelling and working with data marts / warehouses to support dashboard performance.


Dashboarding & Visualization


Hands-on experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools
Ability to support dashboard population, maintenance, and data validation


Cloud & Platform Exposure (Nice to Have)


Exposure to cloud-based data processing environments (AWS or Google Cloud)
Familiarity with version control and structured deployment processes


Soft Skills & Behaviors


Clear communicator with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Strong ownership and accountability for end-to-end data delivery
High attention to detail with a strong data quality mindset
Ability to prioritise and manage multiple tasks in a deadline-driven environment
Strong analytical, problem-solving skills and Comfortable working in global, cross-functional teams


Life at Inizio Ignite | Research Partnership:

We love what we do and continuously strive for innovation and creativity. We believe everyone should continuously be learning and provide:


A comprehensive training and development programme for all staff, from entry level apprentice to senior management
Opportunities for international travel and collaboration
A relaxed and friendly working environment


Further information:

This is a full-time role and is available in a hybrid pattern, based at our fantastic Inizio Advisory location in Gurugram. This role’s working hours will be 13:30 to 22:30 from Monday to Thursday and 11:30 to 20:30 on Friday.

Any specific information that you would want us to gather from the candidates.

Technical:


Experience with Python
Experience with PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MS SQL
Experience managing end-to-end data pipelines
Experience with data warehousing / data marts


Capabilities:


Ability to work autonomously with limited direct oversight / instruction
Client-facing capabilities to explain data management challenges/requirements
Working with a globally distributed team


Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.

title: Customer Service Representative - St Marys
description:
Please note that rosters are predetermined. Before applying please ensure you are available to cover the following roster 

Part Time Roster (12 hours per week):


Monday: 11.30am - 3.30pm
Tuesday: OFF
Wednesday: OFF
Thursday: 11.30am - 3.30pm
Friday: 11.30am - 3.30pm


Do work that matters

As the public face of CommBank, the Retail Banking Services (RBS) team delivers a seamless banking experience to more than 10 million personal and small business customers. We’ve been serving our local communities for over 100 years, with market-leading products, services and technology.

What we do has real impact at all stages of people’s lives, from opening their first account, saving for a holiday, buying their first home, or planning for retirement. Working with us in RBS means you’ll positively impact our customers’ lives, and be there for them when they need us.

See yourself in our team

As a Customer Service Representative in our St Marys Branch you’ll be:


Responding to customers transactional banking needs, delivering a seamless customer experience
Processing transactions including deposits, withdrawals, bank cheques, transfers & CommFX
Completing financial health checks to assess customers’ needs, identify any changes or needs for specific products or services
Assisting with Cash/ATM procedures
Educating and demonstrating of our in-branch technology and digital banking options for customers
Adhering to the Bank’s processes and procedures, including accurately reconciling transactions, reviewing customers’ financial positions & championing our strong risk culture


We’re interested in hearing from people who have:


A genuine passion for Customer Service and are looking to kick-start your career in Banking with this entry-level position
A genuine interest in improving the financial wellbeing and lives of our customers
The ability to ask the right questions, respond to customers’ needs and refer them to the relevant team members in order to better serve their needs
Excellent communication skills & willingness to contribute to a collaborative culture
Ability to work in a busy, commercial and driven retail environment whilst seeing the big picture for customers and providing them with holistic solutions 


What’s Next: As part of our application process, you may be required to complete an online assessment. We'd encourage you to complete these steps within 48 hours of applying.

With us, you’ll help customers make the right financial decisions and achieve their dreams. 

If this role sounds like the perfect fit then we’d love to hear from you. Apply today!

At Commbank, we’re determined to make a real difference for Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander Peoples. You’ll be part of a community that’ll support you professionally and personally, every step of the way. Beyond your team, you’ll also have the opportunity to network across our Indigenous Employee Network, linking to Indigenous employees across CommBank.

If you're already part of the Commonwealth Bank Group (including Bankwest, x15ventures), you'll need to apply through Sidekick to submit a valid application. We’re keen to support you with the next step in your career.

We're aware of some accessibility issues on this site, particularly for screen reader users. We want to make finding your dream job as easy as possible, so if you require additional support please contact HR Direct on 1800 989 696.

Advertising End Date: 27/04/2026
